Same position as Phase1 R175H. Glycine (smallest AA, no side chain) vs Histidine - tests size effect.
Same position as Phase1 R175H. Cysteine introduces thiol group - different chemistry than Histidine.
Same position as Phase1 G245S. Aspartate (charged) vs Serine (neutral) - tests charge effect.
Same position as Phase1 R282W. Glutamine (small) vs Tryptophan (largest AA) - tests size effect.
Same position as Phase1 R248Q/R248W. Leucine (hydrophobic) - tests 3rd substitution at critical DNA contact.
Same position as Phase1 Y220C. Serine vs Cysteine - both small, but different chemistry.
Same position as Phase1 R249S. Glycine (no side chain) vs Serine - tests how minimal substitution compares.
THE most common TP53 polymorphism. Billions of people carry it. NOT cancer-causing. KEY validation control.
Functional polymorphism in African populations. Mild effect on apoptosis but not pathogenic.
Variant of Uncertain Significance. Conservative substitution (Ala→Val). Tests sensitivity threshold.
Brazilian founder mutation. Low cancer risk, pH-dependent tetramerization defect. Borderline case.
Ultra-conservative substitution (Lys→Arg, both positive charge). Should show minimal RMSD if method works.
Tetramerization domain (325-355). Hydrophobic→Charged disrupts oligomerization interface.
Tetramerization domain. Proline is a helix-breaker, should disrupt alpha-helix at tetramer interface.
Transactivation Domain (TAD, 1-61). Affects transcriptional activation, NOT DNA binding.
TAD domain. W23 is ESSENTIAL for MDM2 binding. Disrupts p53 degradation regulation.
Tetramerization domain. Tests conservative substitution in non-DBD domain.
C-terminal regulatory domain. K382 is acetylation site - post-translational regulation region.
Known GOF. Promotes invasion and metastasis. Conservative change (Arg→Lys) but catastrophic effect.
Known GOF. Enhances cancer aggressiveness. Tests if subtle substitutions can still be detected.
Known GOF. Glycine adds backbone flexibility. Tests if increased flexibility = detectable RMSD.
GOF mutation in L3 loop. Large hydrophobic substitution near DNA contact surface.
THE classic temp-sensitive p53 mutant. Inactive at 37°C, active at 32°C. Structurally rescued by cooling.
Temperature-sensitive mutant. Can be reactivated. Tests if structural damage is indeed moderate.
Partial activity mutant near zinc-binding region. Ile→Thr changes hydrophobicity dramatically.
Confirmed pathogenic but rare. Charge reversal (Glu-→Lys+). Outside classic hotspot.
Rare pathogenic in beta-sandwich. Asn→Asp introduces charge. Non-hotspot structural mutation.
DBD non-hotspot. Thr→Met changes polarity. Tests if non-hotspot DBD mutations are less damaging.
Rare pathogenic near zinc-binding. Hydrophobic→charged in structural core. Should be very disruptive.
Adjacent to hotspot R248. Tests if neighboring residue mutations cause comparable damage.
